title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: He would give great consideration to his appearance and smell, and would love perfume. Anas – may Allah be pleased with him – said, “He had the Looking after one’s appearance brightest skin colour. His sweat was pearl-like. When he walked, he leant forward slightly. I have never touched a piece of silk as soft as the hand of the Messenger of Allah . I have never smelt a perfume or an ambergris better than the smell of the Prophet . His hand was perfumed as though he had just withdrawn it from the place where a perfume maker keeps his tools. He was recognised merely by his scent whenever he came.” Anas – may Allah be pleased with him – also said, “The Messenger of Allah  would never refuse perfume. He had the most beautiful face. His face was as bright as the sun. Whenever he became happy, his face brightened, as if it were a piece of moon.” Jabir bin Samurah said, “I once saw the Messenger of Allah  on a night illuminated by a full moon. I compared the Messenger of Allah  to the moon whilst he was wearing a red robe. To me, he seemed more beautiful than the moon.” The Prophet  would encourage the Muslims to look after their appearance. Abul-Haws narrates from his father that he said, “I came to the Prophet  wearing a rough garment. He said, ‘Do you have any wealth?’ ‘Yes’, I said. He said, ‘What do you have?’ I said, ‘Camels, cows, cattle, horses and slaves.’ He said, ‘If Allah gives you wealth, then let the affects of Allah’s bounty and honour upon you be shown in your appearance.’” The Prophet  said, “Whoever Allah blesses with a bounty, Looking after one’s appearance Allah likes to see the affects of that bounty appear on His servant.” Jabir bin ‘Abdullah – may Allah be pleased with him – said, “Once the Messenger of Allah  came to our house for a visit and saw a man with dishevelled hair. He said: ‘Couldn’t he ﬁnd anything to comb his hair with?’” On another occasion he saw a man wearing dirty clothes, and said, “Couldn’t he ﬁnd water to wash his clothes with?” He  also said, “Whoever has hair should honour it.” He used to encourage people to demonstrate good behaviour and have a beautiful appearance, nice clothes and a ﬁne smell. He often used to say, “Allah is beautiful and loves beauty.” (Muslim) Experience… The ﬁrst look makes up for 70% of the impression people get of you. Looking after one’s appearance (68) Honesty I was once invigilating in an examination room on a Thursday. Although Thursday is not a weekday in Saudi Arabia, we were forced to have an exam on that day due to the large number of subjects we had. A few minutes after the beginning of the exam, a student came in late. The poor lad was looking bewildered. I said to him, “Sorry, you have come late and I cannot let you into the examination room.” He began to beg me to allow him in. I asked him, “Why are you late?” He said, “By Allah, dear teacher, I overslept!” I was impressed at his honesty and said, “OK. You may come in.” He entered and sat his exam. A few minutes later another student came late. I said to him, “Why are you late?” He said, “By Allah, dear teacher, there is so much trafﬁc on the road! As you know, everyone leaves for work in the morning – some are going to the university, others are heading for work, etc.” He then began mentioning all the different people on road to assure me that there really was heavy trafﬁc on the road, but the wretched lad forgot that it was a Thursday and that it was therefore a weekend. Perhaps there was nobody at all on the road that day except students from our university! I said to him, “So there was a trafﬁc jam and the road was packed with cars?” He said, “Yes, by Allah, dear teacher! If you were with me you would know what I mean!” I said to him, “You sly person! If you want to lie, then at least plan it properly! Dear brother, today is a Thursday!” Meaning, a weekend - no one goes to work on a Thursday! What trafﬁc are you talking about? He said, “Oh! Sorry, dear teacher! I forgot to say that my tyre was punctured.” Meaning, one of his tyres was punctured so he got delayed while ﬁxing the tyre.The poor lad was thrown, so I just laughed and let him into the examination room. Yes! How horrible it is for people to ﬁnd out that you are lying to them! Lies repel people from you and affect your friendships. It makes people feel that you can never again be trusted.
title_id: 5. Apakah Anda ingin bertemu dengannya di luar lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Beliau sangat memperhatikan penampilan dan aromanya, dan sangat menyukai parfum. Anas radhiyallahu anhu berkata, “Beliau memiliki warna kulit yang paling cerah. Keringatnya seperti mutiara. Ketika berjalan, beliau sedikit condong ke depan. Saya tidak pernah menyentuh sepotong sutra sehalus tangan Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam. Saya tidak pernah mencium parfum atau ambergris yang lebih baik dari aroma Nabi shallallahu alaihi wa sallam. Tangannya harum seolah-olah baru saja diangkat dari tempat pembuat parfum menyimpan peralatannya. Beliau dikenali hanya dari aromanya ketika datang.” Anas radhiyallahu anhu juga berkata, “Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam tidak pernah menolak parfum. Beliau memiliki wajah yang paling indah. Wajahnya bersinar seperti matahari. Setiap kali beliau gembira, wajahnya bersinar, seolah-olah seperti sepotong bulan.” Jabir bin Samurah berkata, “Saya pernah melihat Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam pada suatu malam yang diterangi bulan purnama. Saya membandingkan Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam dengan bulan saat beliau mengenakan jubah merah. Bagi saya, beliau tampak lebih indah dari bulan.” Nabi shallallahu alaihi wa sallam mendorong para Muslim untuk menjaga penampilan mereka. Abul-Haws meriwayatkan dari ayahnya bahwa dia berkata, “Saya datang kepada Nabi shallallahu alaihi wa sallam mengenakan pakaian kasar. Beliau bersabda, ‘Apakah kamu memiliki harta?’ ‘Ya,’ kataku. Beliau bersabda, ‘Apa yang kamu miliki?’ Saya berkata, ‘Unta, sapi, ternak, kuda, dan budak.’ Beliau bersabda, ‘Jika Allah memberimu kekayaan, maka biarlah pengaruh karunia dan kehormatan Allah kepadamu terlihat pada penampilanmu.’” Nabi shallallahu alaihi wa sallam bersabda, “Barang siapa yang Allah berkahi dengan suatu karunia, Allah suka melihat pengaruh karunia itu tampak pada hamba-Nya.” Jabir bin Abdullah radhiyallahu anhu berkata, “Suatu ketika Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam datang ke rumah kami untuk berkunjung dan melihat seorang pria dengan rambut acak-acakan. Beliau bersabda: ‘Tidakkah dia menemukan sesuatu untuk menyisir rambutnya?’” Pada kesempatan lain beliau melihat seorang pria mengenakan pakaian kotor, dan bersabda, “Tidakkah dia menemukan air untuk mencuci pakaiannya?” Beliau juga bersabda, “Barang siapa memiliki rambut hendaknya memuliakannya.” Beliau biasa mendorong orang untuk menunjukkan perilaku yang baik dan memiliki penampilan yang indah, pakaian yang bagus, dan aroma yang harum. Beliau sering berkata, “Allah itu indah dan menyukai keindahan.” (Muslim) Pengalaman... Pandangan pertama membentuk 70% dari kesan yang orang dapatkan tentang Anda. Menjaga penampilan (68) Kejujuran Saya pernah mengawasi ruang ujian pada hari Kamis. Meskipun Kamis bukan hari kerja di Arab Saudi, kami terpaksa mengadakan ujian pada hari itu karena banyaknya mata pelajaran. Beberapa menit setelah ujian dimulai, seorang siswa datang terlambat. Anak malang itu tampak kebingungan. Saya berkata kepadanya, “Maaf, kamu datang terlambat dan saya tidak dapat mengizinkanmu masuk ke ruang ujian.” Dia mulai memohon kepada saya agar mengizinkannya masuk. Saya bertanya kepadanya, “Mengapa kamu terlambat?” Dia berkata, “Demi Allah, Pak Guru yang terhormat, saya kesiangan!” Saya terkesan dengan kejujurannya dan berkata, “Baik. Silakan masuk.” Dia masuk dan mengikuti ujiannya. Beberapa menit kemudian, siswa lain datang terlambat. Saya berkata kepadanya, “Mengapa kamu terlambat?” Dia berkata, “Demi Allah, Pak Guru yang terhormat, jalanan sangat macet! Seperti yang Bapak tahu, semua orang berangkat kerja di pagi hari - sebagian pergi ke universitas, yang lain menuju tempat kerja, dan lain sebagainya.” Dia kemudian mulai menyebutkan berbagai orang di jalan untuk meyakinkan saya bahwa memang ada kemacetan lalu lintas yang parah, tetapi anak sial itu lupa bahwa hari itu adalah Kamis dan oleh karena itu merupakan akhir pekan. Mungkin tidak ada siapa pun di jalan hari itu kecuali mahasiswa dari universitas kami! Saya berkata kepadanya, “Jadi ada kemacetan dan jalan penuh dengan mobil?” Dia berkata, “Ya, demi Allah, Pak Guru! Kalau Bapak ikut bersama saya, Bapak akan tahu maksud saya!” Saya berkata kepadanya, “Kamu licik! Kalau mau berbohong, setidaknya rencanakan dengan benar! Saudara yang baik, hari ini adalah hari Kamis!” Artinya, hari libur - tidak ada yang pergi bekerja pada hari Kamis! Kemacetan apa yang kamu bicarakan? Dia berkata, “Oh! Maaf, Pak Guru! Saya lupa mengatakan bahwa ban saya bocor.” Artinya, salah satu bannya bocor sehingga dia terlambat karena memperbaiki ban. Anak malang itu tersudut, jadi saya hanya tertawa dan mengizinkannya masuk ke ruang ujian. Ya! Betapa mengerikannya ketika orang mengetahui bahwa Anda berbohong kepada mereka! Kebohongan menjauhkan orang dari Anda dan mempengaruhi persahabatan Anda. Kebohongan membuat orang merasa bahwa Anda tidak bisa lagi dipercaya.
